chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ تَعَجَّلَ مِنَ الْمُزْدَلِفَةِ قَبْلَ الْفَجْرِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 170 – The one who hastens from Al-Muzdalifa before the dawn

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/135)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/135)
text: رَجُلٍ وَقَفَ مَعَ النَّاسِ بِجَمْعٍ ثُمَّ أَفَاضَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُفِيضَ النَّاسُ قَالَ إِنْ كَانَ جَاهِلًا فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَفَاضَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمُ شَاةٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Ali Bin Raib, from Misma’a,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, regarding a man who pauses along with the people at Jam’a (place of assembly near Muzdalifah), then he departs before the people depart. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he was ignorant, so there is nothing upon him, and if he had (knowingly) departed before the emergence of the day, so upon him is blood of a sheep (to sacrifice)’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab from Misma’ who has said the following: “This is about a man who stays with people in Jam‘ but leaves it before other people. Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, Tf he is ignorant, he is not obligated for anything in this matter. If he leaves before dawn he owes one sheep.”’
